item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷 Pakistan mediates US-Iran peace talks as uranium dispute persists
item.description: Iran's foreign minister met his Pakistani counterpart to discuss proposals to end the US-Israeli war. Tehran and Washington remain at odds over uranium stockpile and Strait of Hormuz controls.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇯🇵🇦🇺🇮🇳 Quad foreign ministers to meet in Delhi amid China tensions
item.description: The US, Japan, Australia and India will hold a meeting of Quad foreign ministers in New Delhi on May 26, Japan's foreign ministry announced.

item.title: 🇹🇷⚖️ Turkish court ousts main opposition leader Ozel
item.description: A Turkish court annulled the 2023 party congress that elected opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel, effectively removing him from his position and dealing a blow to President Erdogan's challengers.

item.title: 🇨🇳🛢️ China's Hengli petrochemical empire faces US sanctions chill
item.description: The Hengli Group, one of China's largest private oil refiners, has been thrust into the center of a geopolitical power struggle as US sanctions target the company.

item.title: 🌏🤝 APEC trade envoys gather in China to discuss supply chain resilience
item.description: Asia-Pacific trade envoys met in China to discuss multilateral cooperation and supply chain resilience amid global shocks including the US-Iranian conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇱 Trump announces additional 5,000 US troops for Poland
item.description: President Trump declared the US will send 5,000 additional troops to Poland, reversing recent administration moves to reduce European deployments. The announcement stirred confusion amid contradictory statements about US troop presence in Europe.

item.title: 🇧🇾🇺🇦 Belarus will not be dragged into Ukraine war, Lukashenko says
item.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko dismissed any notion Belarus would join Russia in the Ukraine war, but stated Belarus and Russia would jointly defend themselves if attacked. The statement comes amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: 🇱🇻🇱🇹 Latvia and Lithuania issue drone alerts, NATO jets scramble
item.description: Lithuania and Latvia detected drones in their airspace and urged residents to seek shelter while NATO fighter jets scrambled to intercept. This marks the latest in a series of security incidents in the Baltic states.

item.title: 🏛️🇺🇸 US House Republicans cancel Iran war powers vote
item.description: House Republicans cancelled a planned vote on Iran war powers, reflecting internal divisions over congressional authorization for military action. The move comes amid ongoing US-Iran negotiations.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚡ US sanctions nine Hezbollah-aligned individuals in Lebanon
item.description: The United States imposed sanctions on nine individuals with alleged Hezbollah connections in Lebanon, continuing pressure on the Iran-backed group amid broader regional conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Postpones AI Executive Order Citing China Competition
item.description: President Donald Trump postponed signing an executive order on artificial intelligence, stating he did not like certain aspects and did not want to take steps that might undermine the US position in its AI competition with China.

item.title: 🇺🇦 US Approves $108M Hawk Missile System Sale to Ukraine
item.description: The US State Department approved a potential $108.1 million foreign military sale to Ukraine for Hawk missile system maintenance, spare parts and logistics services, bolstering Kyiv's air defense capabilities.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish Opposition Leader Vows to Stay After Court Ousts Him
item.description: Turkey's main opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel vowed not to leave party headquarters after a court ruling removed him from his position, deepening the country's political crisis ahead of crucial elections.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran Claims Military Control Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran published a map claiming "armed forces oversight" across 22,000 sq km of the strategic waterway, extending into UAE and Omani waters. The UAE rejected the claims as "fragments of dreams," while the US directed 94 ships to alter course since the blockade began.

item.title: 🇬🇱🇺🇸 Greenlanders Protest Outside New US Consulate in Nuuk
item.description: Hundreds gathered in the capital chanting "Greenland is for Greenlanders" as the US opened a new consulate amid Trump's push for control over the territory. Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen boycotted the opening ceremony.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Alberta to Hold Referendum on Separating from Canada
item.description: The oil-rich province will vote October 19 on whether to remain in Canada or begin a binding independence process. Premier Danielle Smith said she will vote to stay, but separatists gathered over 300,000 signatures demanding the vote.

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸 Rubio Declares Cuba National Security Threat After Castro Indictment
item.description: The US Secretary of State called Cuba a "leading sponsor of terrorism" and said diplomatic resolution was unlikely. Cuba's foreign minister accused Rubio of trying to "instigate military aggression" as tensions escalate following the indictment of former President Raúl Castro.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Deports All Foreign Activists from Gaza Flotilla
item.description: Israel deported all foreign activists seized from a Gaza-bound aid flotilla after Israeli forces intercepted the vessel. The move follows international condemnation after an Israeli minister mocked the detained activists.

item.title: 🇨🇿 Czech President Warns Europe: Ukraine Is Not a Charity Case
item.description: Czech President Petr Pavel opened GLOBSEC 2026 with a warning against a "bad peace" for Ukraine and called for European procurement reform, arguing that Ukraine serves as Europe's defense laboratory.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Supreme Leader orders Iran keep enriched uranium, hardening stance in talks
item.description: Ayatollah Khamenei issued a directive that Irans near-weapons-grade uranium should not be sent abroad, according to senior Iranian sources. This directly counters a key US demand in ongoing peace negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump vows US will retrieve Irans enriched uranium despite rejection
item.description: President Donald Trump pledged the United States will eventually recover Irans stockpile of highly enriched uranium, countering Tehrans refusal to hand over the material. The standoff remains a key obstacle in peace talks.

item.title: 🇺🇳 Rubio to confront NATO allies over lack of support in Iran war
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet NATO allies for the first time since President Trump questioned the alliance over divisions on the Iran war. The meeting comes amid troop drawdowns from Europe.

item.title: 🏛️ Reuters investigation: Trumps diplomacy unravels as allies forge own paths
item.description: Donald Trumps threats, personal envoys and hollowed-out embassies are reshaping US diplomacy. Allies from Europe to Asia are ignoring presidential rhetoric and building independent diplomatic channels.

item.title: 💰 Asian currencies hit record lows as oil shock hits region
item.description: Asias policymakers are taking urgent steps to shore up economies on the front line of the global energy supply shock. Currencies are falling to record lows and pressure is forcing up interest rates.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel launches new AI battlefield operations division
item.description: The IDF announced the creation of "Alumot," a new division integrating AI into battlefield operations, combining combat soldiers with technology professionals.
